attributes.description: Alerts when any DAG files fail to import (`airflow.dag_processing.import_errors > 0`). Import errors prevent the affected DAGs from running.
attributes.name: [Airflow OTel] DAG import errors present

investigation_guide.blob:
## Airflow DAG import errors present

### What fired
`airflow.dag_processing.import_errors` is greater than zero.

### Why it matters
A DAG that fails to import is invisible to the scheduler — its runs will not be created, and downstream systems that expect those runs will silently miss them.

### Triage
1. Check the Airflow webserver "Import errors" page for the traceback.
2. Correlate with recent deploys or config pushes to the DAG folder.
3. Look at `airflow.dag_processing.total_parse_time` and processor logs for environmental issues.

### Remediation
- Fix or revert the offending DAG file.
- If an external dependency is missing in the scheduler environment, install it and restart the scheduler.

### Tuning
- This rule fires on any non-zero value. Do not raise the threshold — every import error should be triaged.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-airflow.otel-default*
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "airflow.otel"
| WHERE airflow.dag_processing.import_errors IS NOT NULL
// Use max gauge value in window; import_errors is a gauge
| STATS max_errors = MAX(airflow.dag_processing.import_errors)
| WHERE max_errors > 0
